mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth that supports corporate revenue trajectories, producing positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side bottlenecks that trigger short-term risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative following consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO during tightening periods (t-stat > -1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 4.0 percent forces aggressive central bank rate hikes that compress equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns show positive mean performance when CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.0, contradicting the multiple compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate consumer spending capacity, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day forward returns exhibit a negative or zero correlation with PAYEMS growth over any 3-year window (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and rising retail sales indicates unsustainable consumption financed by debt, triggering mean-reversion trades that produce negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 1.0% and RSAFS month-over-month change > 2.0%
failure_condition: The specified GDPC1-RSAFS divergence pattern fails to predict negative 20-day S&P 500 returns with statistical significance (p-value > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid expansion in industrial output during disinflationary periods signals resolving supply constraints that boost risk appetite, driving positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain flat or negative following strong INDPRO expansions during disinflationary phases, invalidating the risk-on transmission channel.
